body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: NTS4PTP - Key Management System for the Precision Time Protocol Based on the Network Time Security Protocol
Network Time Security for the Precision Time Protocol

that means it's not possible to secure against it um against a delayed signal without a non-gnss two-way sync the reason for that is if you imagine we have if we start with our GPS satello I hopefully you can see my cursor if we submit a signal and there's a there's an adversary in the middle of that signal this could be a basic repeater just literally listen and broadcast the receiver will perceive that the clock is lagging so we have a one-way signal there's no way for me to get around this the only way to do this is with an out-of-band a two-way signal and if I have that signal that I can certify that that my delay is founded based off of that that Network traffic and um so um the the current state of the art for gnss authentication uses an algorithm called Tesla which stands for time deficient stream loss tolerant Authentication the reason why we have to use this algorithm is because gnss signals generally have extremely well bandwidth we're talking 50 bits per second so the way that Tesla works is it's constructed in a specific Cadence where we send messages we submit a commit of those messages and then we distribute a key later okay so message commitment key message commitment key and the delay is what allows us to provide that authentication because the construction of these keys are in a specific way we can provide cryptographic authenticity uh in my uh paperwork I labeled this delay Capital Theta the key release delay so this is the time from this commitment to the release of key I have colors on here to demonstrate that so the typical um delay that we're talking about here is it depends on the gnss system uh Galileo is Europe's answer to GPS and Chimera is um is is a future GPS concept that's in the works in the next couple of years to authenticate the signal so on that the the key release delay will be 30 seconds to two minutes or two to six seconds depending on your system mode I specifically work on sbas as fast is the constellation that is um that ensures that aircraft are notified within six seconds of GPS failure we have listening stations throughout the continent that are listening to the GPS signal if there's ever an issue um they're immediately notified via this constellation within six seconds and all of my diagrams I go from left to right for increasing time release so we started with the messages and stuff here's the problem with clock lag so when we have a Tesla system once the key is made public uh people can make forged messages so in order for Tesla to be secure we need to um provide a found on the clock offset so on screen I have my my normal message Cadence and then here is a delayed message Kingdom so we have an adversary that's delaying the signal if they're able to delay the signal more than Capital Theta then they can listen for the key and then Forge messages so this diagram we'd love to write an increasing time of release so my receiver is preceding time and it delayed state uh so far delayed that the key has been released the adversary use is the key to generate the messages and now we have a forged signal this happens if my clock offset lowercase data is less than negative big Theta so if my if if my receiver clock were seven seconds behind for the SBS system then my receiver would accept forgeries so um why do we have this vulnerability so um in the original Tesla scheme there is a bootstrap procedure where the key release delay is set individually by provider and receiver so it's Tesla is an Internet of Things protocol okay two two providers and receivers can can can decide this key release delay and if an adversary interferes with that bootstrap then the key release delay increases so ineffective denial Services possible forgery is not but with GPS Tesla the Q release delay has to be system-wide and a multicast a multicast context so we we can't have individual settings everybody has to have a clock that's within a specific offset individual clocks also have stochastic drifts and knowledge of that insecure clock would reveal whether or not the receiver will accept forgery so if if my clock spontaneously goes to an offset of more than seven seconds or more than six seconds and somebody knows about it then I know that that specific receiver will except for trees and in my paper I show that there's a simple test to find in secret outliers very very simple um I will go really quickly through the slide but essentially if if I'm using my basic ntp protocol and I'm intercepting that ntp message in the clear um I can just measure uh the the receipt time um using top of the T1 inside the timestamp I can infer whether or not a specific clock is delayed with high probability and so these these equations just describe that test but if I can listen to the traffic and the T1 inside MTP is in the clear then I should be able to find those outliers so um so here's the vision of the future of why this is important so when we have autonomous cars and autonomous aircraft soon we're going to have Tesla enabled gnss authentication and requirements are going to come in to say you need to have an authentic signal for your safety of line applications so if you're driving autonomous car you better be authenticating your GPS signal so each of those uh vehicles are going to need to speak with a Time server to to certify the delay bound that I've talked about before and we can have an adversary that's affecting men in the middle times aircraft the context for aircraft is that receiver manufacturers for aircraft do not want an internet connection so what that's going to look like is we'll have a nice clock on the aircraft and then through periodic maintenance we'll we'll do we'll do synchronization but for autonomous vehicles I anticipate that you know the honest vehicle can just synchronize whatever it wants so for an attacker the attacker only needs to wait and find a a bad vehicle so um you know if I I might not be particularly I might not particularly care about which specific Vehicles is bad but if once I find a vehicle I can drive it off the road um this type of spoofing is actually now
